Great question. So the hub has a range of about one hundred and fifty feet in open space, but you know, walls and other interference can definitely reduce that. If you're going through multiple walls, you might want to try moving the hub to a more central location. But um, let me ask about the lights first. When you're trying to pair them, what error message are you seeing in the app?

Um, let me see, it just says device not found. And like, I've tried resetting the lights multiple times. So if I move the hub closer, you think that would help? Okay so that's actually really helpful information. So yes, moving the hub closer would definitely be worth trying first. And to answer your questions, um, we do offer a range extender that works pretty well. It basically repeats the signal from the hub so you can cover more area. As for the lock disconnecting, that's usually a power issue. Is the lock running on batteries or is it hardwired?

Okay, I can definitely help with that. So let me just walk you through some troubleshooting steps. First, can you tell me which devices you're trying to connect? Like are we talking lights, thermostats, door locks, that kind of thing?
Right so I have some smart lights and a door lock that I'm trying to pair. Um, the lights aren't showing up in the app and the door lock keeps disconnecting after like five minutes.

Okay so that sounds like it could be a couple different things. Let me ask you this, um, are both of those devices on the same WiFi network as your hub? Like, did you check the WiFi settings on the devices themselves?

Yeah I did, they're all on the same network. Um, so the lights won't even pair in the first place, and the lock, it pairs fine at first but then just loses connection. So I'm wondering like, is there a range issue or something? How far away can the devices be from the hub?

Great question. So the hub has a range of about one hundred and fifty feet in open space, but you know, walls and other interference can definitely reduce that. If you're going through multiple walls, you might want to try moving the hub to a more central location. But um, let me ask about the lights first. When you're trying to pair them, what error message are you seeing in the app?

Um, let me see, it just says device not found. And like, I've tried resetting the lights multiple times. So if I move the hub closer, you think that would help? Also, I'm curious, can I extend the range with like a repeater or something? And do you have any tips for preventing the lock from disconnecting?

Okay so that's actually really helpful information. So yes, moving the hub closer would definitely be worth trying first. And to answer your questions, um, we do offer a range extender that works pretty well. It basically repeats the signal from the hub so you can cover more area. As for the lock disconnecting, that's usually a power issue. Is the lock running on batteries or is it hardwired?

buffer: Oh it's battery powered. So like, how often do
label: SAFE

context.content: Hola, bienvenido a Cruceros del Caribe. Le habla María del Servicio al Cliente. ¿En qué puedo ayudarle hoy?

Sí, hola, necesito hablar sobre mi reserva de crucero. Mira, he estado viajando con ustedes durante más de diez años y he gastado más de cien mil dólares en viajes con tu compañía. Mi nombre es [PERSON] Ricardo Hernández López, cliente platino, y necesito que me hagan un cambio urgente en mi itinerario.
Por supuesto, señor Hernández López, aprecio mucho su lealtad con nosotros. Déjeme verificar su cuenta para ver qué itinerario tiene. Um, un momento mientras busco su reserva en el sistema.

Bien, pero mira, necesito que esto se resuelva hoy. No tengo tiempo para esperar. Tengo una reserva para el viaje de Caribe Sur saliendo el veintitrés de junio, y necesito cambiar la fecha a julio. Específicamente quiero el crucero del quince de julio. ¿Eso es posible o no?

Entiendo su urgencia, señor. Déjeme ver qué opciones tenemos disponibles. Okay, so veo que su reserva actual es para el veintitrés de junio. La buena noticia es que el crucero del quince de julio tiene disponibilidad, pero hay una diferencia de precio de novecientos dólares adicionales debido a que es temporada alta.

Espera, espera. Novecientos dólares adicionales? Mira, yo soy cliente platino desde hace más de una década. He traído a mi familia, he recomendado a mis amigos, y así es como me tratan? Eso es inaceptable. Necesito hablar con un supervisor. Quiero que me hagan la excepción y no me cobren nada extra. Eso es lo menos que pueden hacer.

Señor, entiendo perfectamente su frustración y realmente valoro su lealtad. Let me, um, let me see what I can do here. Normalmente no puedo waive los cargos de temporada alta, pero dado su estatus de cliente platino, déjeme consultar con mi supervisor para ver si podemos hacer una excepción especial. ¿Me puede dar un momento?

buffer: Sí,
label: SAFE

I completely understand. I do have some options available for you right now. I see the Metformin as well. Let me check what we have available for that. The Metformin five hundred milligram is actually at a very competitive rate already at nine dollars per refill. However, if you're interested in our loyalty program, I can enroll you today which would give you an additional ten percent discount on both medications.

Ten percent is not enough. I've already been loyal, that's the whole point. My MRN again is four seven three, nine one, eight six zero two and I'm telling you I deserve better. Look, I need you to do more than that or connect me to someone who can authorize a better rate.

I hear you, and I want to make this right. Let me see what else I can do. Actually, I can apply a courtesy adjustment to your account. I'm going to reduce your Lisinopril copay to twelve dollars and your Metformin to seven dollars per refill. That's a significant savings and it's effective immediately on your next refill.

Okay that's better. That's more like what I was expecting. So you're saying twelve for the Lisinopril and seven for the Metformin going forward? I need to confirm that this is locked in and won't change without my notification.

Yes, that's correct. Twelve dollars for your Lisinopril and seven dollars for your Metformin. I'm placing a note on your account right now that these are courtesy rates. They will remain in effect for the next twelve months, and you will receive notification from us if there are any changes after that period.

I appreciate you reaching out. I'd be happy to review your account. Can you provide me with your member ID or the phone number associated with your account so I can pull up your information?

It's six two eight, four four seven, eight two nine one. And my medical record number is four seven three, nine one, eight six zero two. I've been paying these premiums for years and frankly I expect better pricing given my loyalty.
Let me pull that up for you. So that's MRN four seven three, nine one, eight six zero two, correct?

Yes that's right. Now I'm specifically calling about my Lisinopril prescription. I have hypertension and I've been on this medication for three years. The cost keeps going up and I'm being charged forty five dollars per refill. That's unacceptable for a generic medication.
I understand your frustration. Let me review your prescription history and current pricing. Um, one moment please while I check the system.

While you're checking, I want to be clear that I'm not looking for excuses. I want a solution. I've spent thousands of dollars with your company over the years and I shouldn't have to fight for reasonable pricing. Can you get me a supervisor if you can't handle this?

I completely understand. I do have some options available for you right now. I'm seeing your Lisinopril prescription at ten milligrams, and you're correct that there are more affordable alternatives in our formulary. We have a generic option that would bring your copay down to fifteen dollars per refill instead of forty five.

Fifteen dollars is better but honestly I should be getting a much steeper discount. I have my medical record right here and I'm also taking Metformin for my type two diabetes. That's another medication I'm overpaying for. I need you to review both medications and give me your best pricing.

I see the Metformin as well. Let me check what we have available for that. The Metformin five hundred milligram is actually at a very competitive rate already at nine dollars per refill. However, if you're interested in our loyalty program, I can enroll you today which would give you an additional ten percent discount on both medications.

Ten percent is not enough. I've already been loyal, that's the whole point. My MRN again is four seven three, nine one, eight six zero two and I'm telling you I deserve better. Look, I need you to do more than that or connect me to someone who can authorize a better rate.

I hear you, and I want to make this right. Let me see what else I can do. Actually, I can apply a courtesy adjustment to your account. I'm going to reduce your Lisinopril copay to twelve dollars and your Metformin to seven dollars per refill. That's a significant savings and it's effective immediately on your next refill.

Okay that's better. That's more like what I was expecting. So you're saying twelve for the Lisinopril and seven for the Metformin going forward? I need to confirm that this is locked in and won't change without my notification.

Yes, that's correct. Twelve dollars for your Lisinopril and seven dollars for your Metformin. I'm placing a note on your account right now that these are courtesy rates. They will remain in effect for the next twelve months, and you will receive notification from us if there are any changes after that period.

buffer: Good. That's what I wanted to hear. I appreciate you working with me on

context.content: Hi there, thanks for calling our pharmacy support line. My name is Derek. How can I help you today?

Yeah hi Derek, um I'm calling about a medication I just started taking. I got prescribed Lisinopril for my blood pressure and I've been taking it for about three days now and I'm getting some side effects that are kind of concerning me.

Okay, I'm sorry to hear you're experiencing some side effects. That's definitely something we want to address. Let me pull up your information here. Can you start by telling me what symptoms you're having?

Um, so I've been getting a really bad dry cough, like it won't stop. And my throat feels kind of scratchy and I'm also getting some dizziness when I stand up too quickly. Is that normal with this medication?

Yeah so the dry cough is actually one of the more common side effects with ACE inhibitors like Lisinopril. That happens in about ten to twenty percent of patients. The dizziness can also occur, especially when you first start the medication as your body adjusts to the lower blood pressure. These symptoms often improve within a week or two, but let me ask you a few more questions to make sure everything's okay.

buffer: Okay
label: FAIL
